titleStmt.title: Letter from John Pedder to the Under-Secretary, 2 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Pedder

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Sir John Pedder, (1869-1956), assistant secretary to the Home Secretary (Herbert Louis Samuel (1870-1963)), to Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), Under-Secretary for Ireland, informing him that Sir John Grenfell Maxwell (1859-1929), Commander-in-Chief of Ireland, has no objection to Dr. Kathleen Lynn (1874-1955) being permitted to remain in Ireland and released from her undertaking to stay in Bath, England.Enclosed is a letter from HQ Irish Command, Parkgate, Dublin to the Chief Commissioner, D.M.P. advising of the updated position. Kathleen Lynn was the chief medical officer in the City Hall garrison during the Easter Rising of 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13503

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
HOME OFFICE WHITEHALL August 2nd, 1916 Sir With reference to your telegram of the 27th July in
the case of Dr. Kathleen Lynn, I am directed by the Secretary
of State to say that in view of the General Maxwell's opinion
that there is no great objection to this lady's remaining
in Ireland he has released her from the undertaking which
she gave to return to Bath as soon as her sister ceased to
require her services and has informed her that she may reâ
main in Ireland. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, John Pedder The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle
URGENT
G/2000/1. HEADQUARTERS, IRISH COMMAND, PARKGATE, DUBLIN. 6th August 1916. CHIEF COMMISSIONER, D. M. POLICE. DOCTOR KATHLEEN LYNN. The Home Secretary has released this lady from her
undertaking to reside in Bath, and informed her that she
may remain in Ireland. Please note for action accordingly. T.I.D Atkinson
CAPTAIN
for D. M. I.,
GENERAL STAFF, IRISH COMMAND.
